summary: ChAMP package is designed for DNA methylation array analysis, providing service from data loading to final gene set enrichment analysis.
ChAMP offers three DMR detection methods: Probe Lasso, Bumphunter, and DMRcate.
The EPIC Simulation Data Set contains 16 samples. In the EPIC Simulation Data Set, 8 samples are simulated as control and 8 samples as case.
In the EPIC Simulation Data Set, approximately 5000 regions were randomly chosen from the bumphunter clusterMaker() function.
The EPIC Simulation Data Set contains less than 5000 DMRs (4700+) because some simulated DMRs contain only 1-2 CpGs.
Three DMR algorithms are implemented in ChAMP: Bumphunter, ProbeLasso, and DMRcate.
Bumphunter groups all probes into small clusters, then applies random permutation method to estimate candidate DMRs.
DMRcate is a data-driven approach that is agnostic to all annotations except for chromosomal coordinates.
champ.DMR() only takes categorical covariates with exactly two phenotypes.
